data.title: ATS Corp reported Q1 fiscal 2026 results: revenue up 6.1% to $736.7 million, net income $24.3 million, EPS 25 cents vs.36 cents previous year.
data.description: ATS Corporation (TSX and NYSE: ATS) in Cambridge, Ontario, released its financial results for the quarter ended on June 29, 2025. All monetary values mentioned are in Canadian dollars. Key highlights from the first quarter include a 6.1% rise in revenue to $736.7 million. The net income for this period stood at $24.3 million, a decrease from $35.3 million in the previous year. Basic earnings per share were reported at 25 cents, down from 36 cents in the comparable period.

data.title: NiCE to release Q2 2025 results on Aug 14, 2025, with conference call at 8:30 AM ET/1:30 PM UK/3:30 PM Israel. Webcast info: https://www.nice.com/company/investors/upcoming-event.
data.description: NiCE (Nasdaq: NICE) is set to reveal its Q2 2025 financial results on August 14, 2025, before NASDAQ Stock Exchange's opening. A post-results conference call hosted by management is scheduled for that day at these times: 8:30 AM Eastern, 1:30 PM UK, and 3:30 PM Israel. The conference call will be live-streamed on the Company's website at https://www.nice.com/company/investors/upcoming-event. Interested parties can register using the provided links for the webcast or dial-in access.

data.title: Somnigroup's Q2 2025 Net Sales up 53%, D2C sales at 66%. Strong performance, raises full-year guidance.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, Somnigroup International Inc. reported a significant 53% growth in net sales. The merger with Mattress Firm is progressing ahead of schedule. Direct sales now account for 66% of net sales, a considerable increase from 23% previously. As a result of these strong performance indicators, the company has raised its financial guidance for the full year 2025.

data.title: ALLETE, Inc. reported Q2 2025 earnings of 55 cents per share on net income of $31.9 million, down from $33.0 million in Q2 2024.
data.description: In Duluth, Minnesota, ALLETE, Inc. (NYSE: ALE) announced their second quarter earnings for 2025, revealing a profit of 55 cents per share with a net income of $31.9 million. This marks a slight decrease from the previous year's second quarter results, which stood at 57 cents per share and a net income of $33.0 million. The net income for the second quarter of 2025 accounts for $3.4 million in after-tax transaction expenses, equivalent to 6 cents per share, associated with a merger that was disclosed. In comparison, the net income for the same quarter in 2024 included $14.5 million in after-tax transaction expenses.

data.title: Transatlantic leaders support new Defence Bank to aid NATO goals; endorsed for 5% GDP defense investment.
data.description: In July, a high-level taskforce led by UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves and Defence Secretary John Healey approved the establishment of the Defence, Security, and Resilience Bank (DSRB). This new international financial institution, endorsed by nations, aims to support NATO and global allies in meeting the significant five percent (5%) of GDP defense investment commitment set during the June 2025 NATO Summit. Additionally, European Parliament Members (MEPs) have also voted in favor of this initiative.
Ghana has declared a three-day mourning period following the death of eight people in a helicopter crash - reports

data.title: NCR Voyix Q2 2025: Revenue $666M, Net income $1M, compared to $722M revenue and $90M net loss in prior year.
data.description: Atlanta-based NCR Voyix Corporation (NYSE: VYX), a top provider of digital commerce solutions, released its financial report for the three and six months ending June 30, 2025. The company reported second-quarter revenue of $666 million, a decrease from $722 million in the same period last year. Net income from continuing operations attributable to NCR Voyix was $1 million, a significant improvement from a net loss of $90 million in the previous year. Adjusted earnings were not disclosed.

data.title: Somnigroup International Inc. (NYSE: SGI) acquires stake in Fullpower-AI, enhancing partnership with a leading biosensing AI provider.
data.description: On August 7, 2025, Somnigroup International Inc. (NYSE: SGI) disclosed an extended partnership with Fullpower-AI®, a key player in biosensing AI solutions. The collaboration, initiated in 2019, involves Fullpower's Sleeptracker-AI technology. This move signifies a strategic growth for the Company in harnessing advanced AI capabilities for enhanced sleep monitoring.

data.title: Somnigroup (NYSE: SGI) declares $0.15/share cash dividend for Q3, payable on Sept 5, 2025.
data.description: Somnigroup International Inc. (NYSE: SGI) based in Dallas, revealed today that it will be issuing a cash dividend of $0.15 per share for the third quarter of 2025. The dividend will be distributed on September 5, 2025.

data.title: Valens Semiconductor (NYSE: VLN) to participate in investor conferences, CFO to attend Oppenheimer 28th Annual event in August.
data.description: Valens Semiconductor, a prominent player in high-performance connectivity based in Hod Hasharon, Israel, disclosed on August 7, 2025, that Guy Nathanzon, the Chief Financial Officer, is set to take part in various investor events this month. The events include the Oppenheimer 28th Annual...

data.title: Immunic, Inc. reports positive results: Vidofludimus Calcium reduces disability worsening in Phase 2 CALLIPER trial by up to 24 weeks.
data.description: Results from the Phase 2 CALLIPER trial show that Vidofludimus Calcium led to a significant decrease in confirmed disability worsening over 24 weeks in patients with progressive Multiple Sclerosis. This effect was seen across all subtypes of the disease, highlighting the drug's neuroprotective properties and its ability to slow down the progression of the condition. The findings suggest the potential of Vidofludimus Calcium in treating Multiple Sclerosis and provide hope for patients seeking therapies to manage the disease.

data.title: Primo Brands reports Q2 2025 results, revises full-year guidance. Cost synergy target $200M in 2025, $300M in 2026. Share buyback $250M. Dividend 10 cents per share.
data.description: The projected net sales, adjusted EBITDA, and adjusted free cash flow for the full year 2025 have been revised. The company has reaffirmed its aim to capture $200 million in cost synergy for 2025 and $300 million for 2026. Additionally, a new share repurchase program worth $250 million has been announced. A quarterly dividend of 10 cents per share has also been declared. The company restated its commitment to certain strategies and targets.

data.title: Nuvalent reports Q2 2025 financial results, starts NDA submission for zidesamtinib in NSCLC patients, and launches Phase 3 trial for neladalkib in ALK-positive NSCLC.
data.description: The process has been started for the gradual submission of a New Drug Application (NDA) for zidesamtinib targeting patients with advanced ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who have been previously treated with tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKI). The submission is expected to be completed by the third quarter of 2025. Additionally, the ALKAZAR Phase 3 randomized, controlled trial has commenced to evaluate neladalkib for the treatment of front-line ALK-positive NSCLC. Further updates and significant data from this pivotal trial are anticipated in the future.

data.title: BD's Q3 fiscal 2025 revenue grew by 10.4% to $5.5 billion. GAAP and adjusted EPS rose to $2.00 and $3.68. Gross margin improved by 160 and 50 basis points, respectively.
data.description: The company saw a rise in revenue to $5.5 billion, marking a 10.4% increase as reported, 8.5% adjusted for FXN, and 3.0% organically. Both GAAP and adjusted diluted earnings per share (EPS) experienced growth, reaching $2.00 and $3.68, respectively. Additionally, the gross margin for both GAAP and adjusted figures increased by 160 basis points and 50 basis points, respectively, due to BD Excellence initiatives.

data.title: Zimmer Biomet reports Q2 2025 net sales of $2.077B, up 7.0%. Diluted earnings per share at $0.77. Adjusted EPS at $2.07, up 3.0%.
data.description: In the second quarter, net sales rose to $2.077 billion, marking a 7.0% increase on a reported basis, 5.4% on a constant currency basis, and 2.8% on an organic constant currency basis. Diluted earnings per share for the quarter stood at $0.77, with adjusted diluted earnings per share reaching $2.07, reflecting a 3.0% increase.

data.title: United Parks & Resorts Inc. (NYSE: PRKS) reports Q2 2025 results: attendance at 6.2 million, strong performance in first six months.
data.description: In a press release issued on August 7, 2025, United Parks & Resorts Inc. (NYSE: PRKS), a prominent theme park and entertainment organization based in Orlando, Florida, shared its financial performance for the second quarter and initial six months of the fiscal year 2025. During the second quarter of 2025, attendance figures stood at 6.2 million visitors.
